meta.name: Mexican Rice (Tex-Mex)
meta.subtitle: Toasty long-grain rice simmered in tomato broth with onion, garlic, and warm spices.

source.name: SSA Canonical Tex-Mex
notes: Built for consistency: toast rice → blend/simmer with tomato broth.
Includes optional peas/carrots (restaurant-style), and an oven-bake fallback.

yield.notes: Makes about 4 to 4 1/2 cups cooked rice.

summary: Fluffy, savory Mexican-style rice with a toasted base and tomato broth. A foundational Tex-Mex side for tacos, enchiladas, fajitas, bowls, and more.

notes: toasty
tomato
garlic_onion
warm_spices

optional.label: Oven-safe covered dish (oven-bake method)
optional.notes: Useful if you prefer oven rice for hands-off reliability.

ingredients.label: Long-grain white rice
ingredients.notes: Rinse if desired; some cooks skip rinsing to keep more toasted flavor.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil (or lard)
ingredients.notes: Lard adds classic flavor; oil keeps it vegetarian/vegan.

ingredients.label: Butter (optional, richness)
ingredients.notes: Optional; use instead of part of the oil for richer rice.

ingredients.label: Tomato sauce
ingredients.notes: 8 oz can is 1 cup; use half and save the rest for sauce or soup.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste
ingredients.notes: Optional for deeper tomato color/flavor; reduce tomato sauce slightly if using a lot.

ingredients.label: Chicken broth (or vegetable broth)
ingredients.notes: Warm broth helps keep rice fluffy.

ingredients.label: Chicken bouillon powder (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds restaurant-style savoriness; adjust salt if using.

ingredients.label: Ground cumin
ingredients.notes: Keep subtle; Tex-Mex rice is typically not heavily spiced.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Start with 1 tsp; broth/bouillon saltiness varies.

ingredients.label: Carrot, small diced (optional)
ingredients.notes: Dice small so it softens during the simmer.

steps.title: Optional: rinse and drain the rice
instructions: If you prefer less starch, rinse rice in a fine-mesh strainer under cool water until it runs mostly clear.
Drain well to avoid splattering when toasting.

steps.title: Toast the rice
instructions: Heat oil (or lard) in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add rice and stir constantly until most grains look lightly golden and smell nutty, 5–7 minutes.
Don’t rush this step; the toast is the key to non-mushy Mexican rice.
checks: Rice lightly golden; nutty aroma; no burnt spots

steps.title: Sauté onion and garlic
instructions: Stir in diced onion and cook 2–3 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant.
checks: Onion softened; garlic fragrant, not browned

steps.title: Build the tomato broth
instructions: Stir in tomato paste (if using) and cook 30 seconds to remove raw taste.
Add tomato sauce, broth, bouillon (optional), cumin (optional), salt, and pepper.
Stir well and bring to a gentle boil.

steps.title: Cover and simmer until rice is tender
instructions: Reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer 18 minutes without lifting the lid.
If using diced carrots, add them now (they’ll soften as the rice cooks).
If adding peas, you can add them at the end (see next step) to keep them bright.

steps.title: Rest, then fluff
instructions: Remove from heat and keep covered for 8–10 minutes (this finishes steaming).
If using peas, sprinkle them on top during the rest so they warm through.
Fluff gently with a fork and taste for salt.
checks: Rice fluffy; grains separate; seasoning balanced

steps.title: Oven-bake method (optional, very reliable)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Toast rice and sauté aromatics in an oven-safe covered dish or skillet.
Add tomato sauce + broth + seasonings, bring to a brief simmer, cover, then bake 25 minutes.
Rest covered 10 minutes, then fluff.
checks: Oven rice fluffy; not wet at bottom

notes: Cool quickly and store airtight.
Reheat with 1–2 tbsp water per cup; cover to steam.
Freeze in flat portions for quick thawing.
Texture is best if reheated covered with a splash of water.
Cool cooked rice quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.
Reheat until steaming hot; avoid holding warm rice for long periods.

batchPrep: Double the recipe for meal prep: portion into 1-cup containers.
Pairs naturally with refried beans for fast bowls and taco nights.

notes: If scaling up, use a wider pot for even toasting and a tight-fitting lid for simmer.
For very large batches, oven-bake method is the most consistent.
Add bouillon powder and a touch more tomato paste.
Use 1/4 tsp cumin max to keep flavor classic and not overly spiced.
Add 1/3 cup small-diced carrots during simmer.
Add 1/2 cup peas during rest to keep them bright.
Use neutral oil and vegetable broth.
Skip chicken bouillon (or use vegan bouillon).
